prose:
Method–provenance summary (all mandatory fields)
• Property: frontier orbital energies (HOMO, LUMO) and HOMO–LUMO gap, in eV  
• Level of theory: density‐functional theory, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting)  
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied ethylene.xyz (6 atoms), taken as-is; single-point calculation (no further relaxation) – notation: DFT//input  
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model)  
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet; RHF → RKS)  
• Software/backend: PySCF (called via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill)  
• Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 6 cycles; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true; no engine warnings were reported.

Live log & files
The run streamed its progress to a live log written as `<cwd>/frontier_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.out` (created automatically by chemkit when the job started).  
Full result JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp8t20dkqz/live.json (contains all fields, command line, and geometry).

Key numerical results (gas-phase, B3LYP/def2-TZVP)
• Total electronic energy  E = −78.623486 Eh  (−2139.4541 eV)  
• HOMO energy             ε_H = −7.6155818 eV  
• LUMO energy             ε_L = −0.17986527 eV  
• HOMO–LUMO gap           Δε_HL = 7.4357165 eV   ← headline value  
• Koopmans vertical IP    7.6155818 eV (−ε_H)  
• Koopmans vertical EA    0.17986527 eV (−ε_L)  

All results are computed; no experimental comparison was requested. No warnings were emitted by the backend, and the calculation is flagged trustworthy by chemkit’s integrity checker.
